name: Draw Card — Retro Gacha Wish Machine
description: A playful Three.js H5 gift built around a retro card-drawing machine. The page opens on a neon lyric rain and a rotating pixel-photo carousel, then lets the recipient fill a short wish card with a lyric, color, style, and memo. After the wish slides into the machine, turning the side knob reveals a collectible image card that can be saved.

slots.purpose: Photos, screenshots, concert notes, fandom jokes, favorite lyrics, colors, nicknames, short phrases, and memo fragments that make the card machine feel made for this recipient instead of a generic fan toy.
grounding_rules: Visible copy and default form text should trace to a concrete lyric, joke, date, concert, color, nickname, or detail from the user's material.
Use the language and address form the user naturally uses with the recipient.
Do not invent a celebrity preference, concert attendance, or relationship inside joke that the user did not provide.

slots.purpose: A compact editorial plan for what the machine is drawing and why the recipient would care.

output_fields.subject_name: who or what the card set is about, e.g. Eason, a friend, a fictional mascot, or a shared concert
output_fields.recipient_address: short address form used in UI copy
output_fields.occasion: why this card machine is being sent now
output_fields.core_lyric_or_phrase: the concrete lyric, phrase, or joke that anchors the gift
output_fields.visual_language: the color, card style, and sticker mood
output_fields.card_emotion: what the drawn card should give back: encouragement, hype, comfort, joke, or keepsake

rules: Make the machine draw one clear emotional object, not a random card pack.
If the material is fandom-oriented, keep the tone playful and consensual rather than pretending personal intimacy with a public figure.
If the material is about a real recipient, keep all default wish text grounded in what the user actually gave.

slots.purpose: Lyric-rain lines that fall behind the machine. They may be user-provided lyrics, short quoted phrases, or original lines composed from the gift brief.
rules: Do not paste long copyrighted lyrics. Use short user-provided lines or original lines when needed.
Keep each line short enough to read while falling across mobile and desktop.

slots.purpose: Images shown in the gacha machine's pixel-photo carousel. Defaults to the bundled Eason reference set, but can be replaced with user-provided photos, concert photos, or generated/proxy images that match the gift.

params.note: Faces and gestures must remain crop-safe after the machine pixelates the carousel.

slots.purpose: Hero photo pool for the generated/saveable cards. The builder can use bundled card_materials or per-gift processed photos.

params.note: Leave enough image area for canvas overlays, paper slips, stickers, and lyric text.

slots.purpose: Optional custom circular mascot stickers used on cards. Defaults to the bundled Eason-style sticker set; use this slot only when the user wants a custom person/mascot/fandom character.
style_rules: Flat circular face or mascot disc, thick black rounded doodle lines, transparent PNG, no realistic portrait rendering.
Keep the sticker set internally consistent across expressions and colors.
Do not generate a real person's exact identity unless the user provided suitable reference photos and consent context.
slots.model_hint: Use a general image model for custom stickers; bundled stickers require no generation.

slots.selection_hint: Pick small stickers that match the user's cue: stage/concert, travel, sweet drink, soft blue night, playful joke, birthday sparkle, or movie-ticket energy.
placement_rules: Decor stickers should support the card style and lyric; avoid unrelated sticker scatter.
Do not cover the subject's eyes, the main lyric text, or the generated card's saveable center.
If the card style is quiet or letter-like, use fewer stickers and lower opacity.

slots.purpose: Gift-facing UI copy and default wish-card fields. This is part of the gift, not documentation.

output_fields.copy.memoPlaceholder: example keywords in the user's language
output_fields.copy.machineButton: short text drawn on the 3D machine button
output_fields.copy.delivered: toast after the wish card enters the slot
output_fields.wishDefaults.lyric: default lyric or phrase on the request card

slots.language_rule: Use the user's habitual language, writing system, nickname, and warmth level for final gift text.
specificity_rules: Default text should include or echo a concrete lyric, phrase, color, place, date, or joke from the recipient material.
Avoid broad labels such as 'best wishes', 'happy forever', or 'special memories' unless the user actually uses those words.

slots.purpose: Optional precise runtime overrides for media lists, text copy, style options, card templates, sticker groups, and default form values.
slots.schema_note: Maps into window.DRAW_CARD_GIFT_CONFIG. Use carouselPhotos, wishPhotos, wishStickers, decorStickers, decorStickerGroups, easonStyleOptions, cardStyleOptions, wishStyleCandidates, cardTemplates, copy, and wishDefaults.
slots.bounds_rule: When replacing card templates, keep lyric, memo, photo, and sticker elements inside the 512x728 saveable card canvas.

build_contract.input: filled-slots.json containing draw_card_copy/wish_copy, lyrics, selected media lists, custom stickers, and optional runtime overrides
build_contract.output: single index.html in the gift folder
build_contract.source_policy: template-source/retro-gacha-card.html is canonical and read-only; build.py writes a separate output file.
build_contract.inline_strategy: base64 local image/script paths from filled-slots.json, the workdir, the fetched base bundle, or template-source into the output HTML

activation_disclosure.message: 这个模板会下载约 24MB 的卡片照片、贴纸和歌词素材；默认能直接做 Eason 风格抽卡机。如果要换成专属人物/角色，可能需要额外图片处理或贴纸生成。
activation_disclosure.show_before: fetching asset bundle or generating custom stickers
cost_notes: Asset bundle download is about 24MB on first use.
Full custom card sets may use image generation for mascot stickers or replacement card photos.
The authored H5 uses browser CDN libraries for React, Three.js, GSAP, Tailwind, and Babel; browser verification needs network unless those libraries are vendored later.
Final gift text should be written in the user's habitual language with the recipient.

options.label: built-in Eason card machine
options.description: Download the bundle, use the bundled photos/stickers/lyrics, customize copy and defaults, and build the interactive H5.

options.label: custom card set
options.description: Replace photos, lyrics, style chips, and optionally generate custom mascot stickers before building.

options.label: text/image fallback
options.description: Skip the heavy H5 path and make a sendable static card or text gift using the same brief.
